sector.name: Wires & Cables
sector.description: Cable makers riding power, infra and export demand

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue crossed ₹28,500 Cr (+29% YoY); EBITDA grew 35% YoY with 14% margin
FY26 PAT crossed ₹2,700 Cr (+32% YoY), with PAT margin of 9%
Q4FY26 consolidated revenue grew 27% YoY; Q4FY26 EBITDA grew 13% YoY with 13% margin
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹790 Cr (+7% YoY)
FY26 capex stood around ₹1,480 Cr and net cash stood at ₹4,190 Cr
highlights: Polycab delivered its highest-ever annual and quarterly revenue in FY26
Domestic W&C organised market share improved to 30-31% vs 26-27% in FY25 and 18-19% in FY19
FY26 W&C volume growth stood around 18%, ahead of industry growth of around 11-12%
Capacity utilisation stood around 70-75%, giving room for growth
North America contributed around 40% of FY26 exports; South America contributed around 20%
FMEG business remained profitable, with solar products delivering 2x YoY growth
Polycab India Ltd.quote: "Polycab continues to outperform the sector on scale, market share gains, volume growth, profitability, balance sheet strength and capex execution."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹11,746 Cr (+21% YoY); Wires & Cable segment grew 22% YoY
FY26 EBITDA stood at ₹1,387 Cr (+31% YoY), with EBITDA margin of 12% vs 11% last year
FY26 PAT stood at ₹918 Cr, with PAT margin of 8%
Q4FY26 net sales stood at ₹3,476 Cr (+19% YoY); Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹284 Cr (+26% YoY)
Distribution sales stood at ₹6,349 Cr (+25% YoY) and dealer sales contributed 54% of total sales
highlights: FY26 growth was supported by W&C demand, distribution growth, EHV cables and exports
Rajasthan cable plants were already operating at peak capacity
FY26 copper cable volume grew 15%, while aluminium volume remained flat
Sanand Phase 1 was commissioned in December 2025 after a delay of around 6 months
Domestic EHV cable sales stood at ₹559 Cr, up 82%
Export sales stood at ₹1,833 Cr, up 45%; US exports have restarted after a tariff-related lull
KEI Industries Ltd.quote: "KEI is outperforming the sector on exports, EHV cables, distribution growth and capacity-led FY27 visibility."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹9,722 Cr (+28% YoY); EBITDA stood at ₹789 Cr (+62% YoY)
FY26 PAT stood at ₹492 Cr (+58% YoY)
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹2,964 Cr (+34% YoY); Q4FY26 EBITDA stood at ₹264 Cr (+35% YoY)
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹168 Cr (+30% YoY)
Wires & Cables FY26 revenue stood at ₹8,764 Cr (+31% YoY), contributing around 90% of FY26 revenue
highlights: RR Kabel delivered its highest-ever quarterly and annual revenue
Domestic demand remained healthy while export demand was relatively stronger during the quarter
W&C full-year volume growth stood around 16%
Management expects cable volume growth to be higher than wire growth
Project RRise targets 18% CAGR in W&C, 25% CAGR in FMEG and 2.5x cumulative EBITDA growth
FMEG losses reduced and management remains on track to achieve breakeven in FY27
R R Kabel Ltd.quote: "RR Kabel is outperforming the sector on W&C growth, EBITDA growth, profitability improvement and B2B / export execution."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹22,902 Cr (+23% YoY); EBITDA post forex stood at ₹2,067 Cr (+23% YoY)
FY26 PAT stood at ₹977 Cr (+19% YoY), with PAT margin of 4%
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹6,603 Cr (+27% YoY); Q4FY26 EBITDA post forex stood at ₹584 Cr (+19% YoY)
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹254 Cr; adjusted operating PAT stood at ₹285 Cr
Domestic revenue grew 29% in FY26, export revenue grew 12%, and US revenue grew nearly 50%

guidance.value: US, data centres, utilities, renewables and premium conductors

highlights: APAR delivered all-time high annual revenue in FY26
Company scaled revenue from ₹6,406 Cr in FY21 to ₹22,902 Cr in FY26, implying around 29% CAGR over 5 years
Conductor FY26 revenue stood at ₹12,712 Cr (+33% YoY); premium products formed 46% of FY26 conductor revenue
Cable division FY26 revenue stood at ₹6,220 Cr (+26% YoY), with EBITDA of ₹633 Cr and 10% margin
Cable export revenue grew 31% YoY and cable US revenue grew 47% in FY26
Company has already supplied to 3 major US data centre projects worth around $15 mn
Apar Industries Ltd.quote: "APAR is outperforming the sector on US exposure, premium conductor mix, cables growth, data centre opportunity and export-led optionality."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue grew 17% YoY, with operating profit of ₹130 Cr (+23% YoY)
Operating margin stood at 11%; PAT stood at ₹84 Cr (+30% YoY)
Order book stood at ₹808 Cr as of March 2026
FY26 customer mix was 13% government sales, 80% private sales and 7% exports
FY26 product mix was 64% HV cables, 30% LV cables and 6% conductors

guidance.value: E-Beam, renewable, solar and DC cables

highlights: Dynamic Cables delivered highest-ever revenue and profitability in FY26
Growth was driven by disciplined execution, stronger operating performance and better product mix
Q4FY26 overall growth looked softer at 7%, but core product growth was 20%
Company reduced low-value conductors to sub-5% of sales vs 8-9% last year
Railway signalling cables were discontinued because competitive intensity was high and margins were weaker
New capacity implementation was delayed due to approvals and imported machinery disruption linked to West Asia logistics
Dynamic Cables Ltd.quote: "Dynamic Cables is outperforming smaller peers on profitability improvement, product mix shift and value-added cable focus."
